greenhouse_5194176008.title: Assistant General Manager
greenhouse_5194176008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto's Assistant General Manager role in Annapolis sits squarely between the leasing floor and the GM's office. You're not purely a closer, and you're not purely an ops person. You do both, often on the same afternoon. That duality is the whole point of the job.

On the leasing side, you're conducting tours that actually convert. Not scripted walkthroughs, but conversations that help a prospective resident picture their life in the community. Traffic-to-lease ratios matter here. So does retention. You're working to make sure every resident interaction lands well enough that renewals feel like an easy yes rather than a negotiation.

On the financial side, you're in the rent collection process daily. Posting receipts, watching delinquency, keeping accounts receivable tight. You don't need to be an accountant, but you do need to understand why a delinquency spike at month-end is everyone's problem and how to address it before it becomes the GM's headache.

When the GM is out, you're running the show. That means coaching the team, keeping the property's interior and exterior presentation sharp, and solving resident issues without escalating everything upward. The ability to make a confident call without backup is something Bozzuto is specifically looking for here.

Weekend availability is part of the deal. One to two weekends per month, with weekday days off in exchange. That's when prospective residents are actually out looking, so it's when the leasing work gets done. It's not unusual in this business, but it's worth knowing upfront.

What separates strong AGM candidates from average ones at a company like Bozzuto isn't just sales ability. It's the combination of interpersonal range and operational discipline. Plenty of people can give a good tour. Fewer can give a great tour and then go back to the office and clean up a delinquency report without losing momentum. That combination is what this role actually demands.

What you bring

  • Four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years in a client-facing role with some exposure to rent collection, budgets, or accounts receivable
  • Comfort with Yardi or comparable property management software
  • Active presence on social platforms and the ability to create content that reflects a community's character
  • Strong written and verbal communication, and the professional polish to represent the brand on-site

The salary range runs from roughly $72,600 to $75,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone looking to grow toward a General Manager seat, this role is a direct path. Bozzuto promotes from within, and the AGM position is exactly where that track starts.

greenhouse_5191067008.title: Assistant General Manager
greenhouse_5191067008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto runs a tight operation at the property level, and the Assistant General Manager role is where that shows most clearly. You're second in command, which means you're doing the work and leading the team, often at the same time. This position is based at a residential community in Exton, PA.

What This Role Actually Involves

Your days split across leasing, operations, and people management. You'll conduct tours with prospective residents, convert traffic into signed leases, and follow up on delinquency. Rent collection, posting receipts, and managing accounts receivable are part of your regular rhythm, not occasional tasks. You're also responsible for how the property looks, inside and out, and how the team presents itself.

When the General Manager is out, you run the site. That's not a formality. You make decisions, coach team members, and handle resident issues with real authority. Bozzuto expects you to step in without needing a script.

Weekend availability is part of this role. One to two weekends per month, with weekday days off in exchange. Prospective residents shop apartments on weekends, and someone senior needs to be there.

What You Need to Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years in a customer-facing, operations-oriented role
  • Hands-on experience with rent collection, budgets, and accounts receivable
  • Comfort with Yardi or similar property management software
  • Active presence on social media platforms, with the ability to create content that actually connects with people
  • Strong written and verbal communication. You're representing the community in every interaction.
  • A sales instinct. You should be able to read a prospect, build rapport quickly, and close.

Where This Role Can Take You

The AGM seat at a company like Bozzuto is a proving ground. Strong performers here typically move into General Manager roles, which means full P&L ownership, direct oversight of NOI, and accountability for the entire site's performance. The skills you build here, delinquency management, leasing conversion, team leadership under pressure, transfer directly to that next level. If you want to run your own property, this is the track.

What separates a strong AGM candidate from an average one isn't just leasing ability. It's the combination of financial literacy and people instincts. You need to care about occupancy numbers and genuinely enjoy talking to residents. Both matter here, and neither alone is enough.

Compensation for this role falls between $68,000 and $70,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of base pay. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5162715008.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
greenhouse_5162715008.rewritten_description:
Maintenance operations at multifamily properties only work when someone bridges the gap between hands-on trade work and team coordination. That's what this role does. Bozzuto is hiring an Assistant Maintenance Manager at their Atlanta property to keep the building running, the turns moving, and the team accountable.

What the Job Actually Looks Like

You're splitting your time between wrench work and oversight. On any given day, that means diagnosing an HVAC issue in one unit, checking punch list progress on a make-ready down the hall, then following up on a resident service ticket that's been open too long. You're not just fixing things. You're making sure the whole maintenance operation holds its standard.

The role covers the full trades spectrum: HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair, basic carpentry, drywall, and painting. Exposure to complex mechanical systems like central plant, chillers, or boilers is a plus. Preventative maintenance programs are yours to execute consistently, not just check off a list.

The make-ready process is a big piece of this. Turns need to move fast and finish clean so leasing can do its job. You'll manage that flow with a sharp eye for detail and a real sense of urgency.

On-call rotation is part of the deal. Weekends too. That's the job, and strong candidates go in knowing it.

What Bozzuto Needs From You

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC certification, EPA certification, or NAPE certification (at least one)
  • Solid working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management basics
  • Some supervisory experience, or a clear track record of leading peers informally
  • Comfort with property management technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office tools
  • Physical capability to handle the demands of the role: lifting up to 50 pounds frequently, climbing ladders, working outdoors in varying conditions

Pay runs $27 to $29 per hour, with bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

The candidates who stand out in this role aren't just technically sharp. They're the ones who treat a resident's work order with the same seriousness they bring to a boiler inspection. That combination of trade skill and people accountability is what separates a good maintenance tech from someone ready to step into a leadership track. From here, the natural next move is a Service Manager or Maintenance Director role, and Bozzuto's size means those opportunities exist within the same portfolio.

greenhouse_5181089008.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
greenhouse_5181089008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation across the Mid-Atlantic and Pacific Northwest for communities that feel genuinely well-run. That reputation depends heavily on maintenance teams who treat asset preservation as a craft, not a checklist. This Assistant Maintenance Manager role in Issaquah is for someone who brings real trade depth to the work and can translate that depth into a team culture where standards don't slip.

The job sits at the intersection of hands-on technical work and front-line supervision. You're not purely a wrench-turner, and you're not purely a desk manager. On any given day you might be troubleshooting an HVAC issue in the morning, walking a punch list on a make-ready unit at midday, and coaching a maintenance tech on work order documentation before you leave. The make-ready process matters here: vacant units cost money, and the ability to manage turns with precision directly affects occupancy and the leasing team's ability to convert traffic into signed leases.

Preventative maintenance isn't glamorous, but it's where the real asset protection happens. Bozzuto expects you to own that program, not just participate in it. Exposure to complex mechanical systems, whether central plant, boilers, or split systems, is genuinely useful in this building. If you've only ever worked in simple garden-style product, it's worth being honest with yourself about the learning curve here.

The on-call rotation is real. Emergencies don't schedule themselves, and this role participates in that coverage. If that's new information, it shouldn't be. It's standard for any maintenance management role, and the candidates who handle it well are the ones who've already made peace with occasional off-hours calls rather than being surprised by them.

What separates strong candidates from average ones here is the leadership piece. Plenty of people have the technical credentials. Fewer have the instinct to correct a team member's work in a way that actually improves their skills rather than just fixing the immediate problem. Bozzuto's culture puts a premium on that kind of mentorship, and it shows in how they describe this role internally.

What you'll need to bring

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of HVAC, plumbing, electrical systems, and appliance repair
  • Familiarity with OSHA standards and risk management practices
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • Prior supervisory experience, or a demonstrated readiness to lead
  • Comfort with technology platforms and work order management systems
  • Availability for emergency on-call rotation and weekend work
  • Physical ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently and work in varying weather conditions

The salary range runs from $60,000 to just under $67,000, with bonus eligibility, medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone who wants to grow toward a Maintenance Manager or Director of Facilities role, this is a reasonable place to build that track record with an operator that invests in development.

greenhouse_5169124008.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
greenhouse_5169124008.rewritten_description:
Skilled trades knowledge only gets you so far in this role. What actually makes the difference here is the ability to hold a maintenance operation together through your technical depth, your eye for detail, and your capacity to get other people working at the same standard you hold yourself to. This is a hands-on supervisory position, and the balance between doing and leading is constant.

What This Role Demands Day to Day

The work splits into three broad categories: reactive, preventative, and administrative. On the reactive side, you're resolving HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance issues with the kind of competence that comes from real field experience, not just book knowledge. Preventative work means running scheduled inspections and maintenance programs that protect the building's long-term value. The administrative layer is lighter but real: service ticket tracking, code compliance, safety protocols, and enough comfort with technology platforms to stay organized.

The turn process is a significant part of the job. Getting make-ready units through punch list and back to leasable condition on time directly affects occupancy, and Bozzuto operates at a level where that timeline matters. You're not just completing turns yourself. You're making sure the team completes them correctly.

Oakland's housing stock includes a real mix of building vintages, and this role specifically calls out experience with complex mechanical systems: central plant, boilers, chillers, split systems. That's not incidental. Candidates who've only worked garden-style wood-frame product may find the mechanical expectations here a step up.

This position also includes weekend availability and emergency on-call rotation. That's standard for roles at this level, but worth naming plainly. The building doesn't stop needing attention because it's Saturday night.

Skills and Background That Fit

  • Five or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management practices
  • Hands-on proficiency across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • Supervisory experience or strong leadership aptitude
  • Comfort with Microsoft Office and property management technology platforms
  • Physical ability to handle frequent lifting in the 25 to 50 pound range, stair climbing, ladder use, and outdoor work in variable weather

What's Distinctive About This Role at Bozzuto

Bozzuto has a reputation in the managed properties space for running tight operations with genuine attention to resident experience. The "preserve and protect" framing in this role isn't just marketing language. It reflects how the maintenance function is positioned internally: not as a cost center to minimize, but as a direct contributor to resident satisfaction and asset value. For a maintenance professional who takes pride in the physical condition of a property and wants that mindset recognized, that cultural alignment is worth something.

The salary range for this position is $80,000 to $87,000, with bonus eligibility, 20 days of paid time off, medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5186710008.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
greenhouse_5186710008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto operates some of the more well-regarded residential communities in the country, and the Boston property tied to this role reflects that standard. The Assistant Maintenance Manager position sits between hands-on trade work and team leadership. You're not just fixing things. You're setting the pace for everyone around you.

Day to day, you're working through service tickets, keeping HVAC systems, plumbing, and electrical in good shape, and making sure the make-ready process runs tight. Turns need to close fast. Punch lists need to actually get punched. When a unit sits because a make-ready dragged on, that's a leasing problem, and you'll understand that instinctively.

Preventative maintenance is a real part of this job, not an afterthought. You'll own the PM schedule alongside the maintenance manager, track building systems, and stay ahead of issues before residents feel them. Boston winters add complexity here. Snow removal, mechanical system performance under cold stress, and keeping the property clean and safe when conditions get rough are all on your plate.

The on-call rotation is real. So are weekend shifts. If that's a dealbreaker, this role isn't the right fit. But if you're used to that rhythm and you take pride in being the person who shows up when something goes wrong, this is a position where that matters.

What separates strong candidates here isn't just trade knowledge. It's the combination of technical skill and the ability to hold a team accountable without micromanaging. Bozzuto runs a culture-forward operation, and they'll notice quickly whether you lead by example or just talk about it.

What you'll need

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Solid working knowledge of plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Familiarity with complex mechanical systems: boilers, chillers, central plant, or split systems is a plus
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • OSHA awareness and a genuine safety-first mindset
  • Supervisory experience, or a clear track record of leading peers informally
  • Comfort with technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office proficiency
  • Physical ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds regularly and work in varying weather conditions

The salary range is $65,000 to $70,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

This role is a natural stepping stone toward a full Maintenance Manager position. Candidates who use this role to build out their preventative maintenance discipline and sharpen their leadership skills tend to move up within Bozzuto's portfolio relatively quickly.

greenhouse_5193940008.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
greenhouse_5193940008.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role sits between the hands-on work and the management layer, which means your days won't look the same twice. One morning you're troubleshooting an HVAC unit that stopped cooling overnight. By afternoon you're walking a make-ready with your team, checking punch list items before a new resident moves in. The job demands both technical depth and the ability to get other people moving in the right direction.

Bozzuto operates with a "preserve and protect" mentality, and that philosophy shapes the daily rhythm here. You'll handle service tickets across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair, and you'll be expected to close them out with real follow-through, not just a checkbox. The make-ready process is a core part of this position. Turns need to be tight. New residents notice everything, and a sloppy turn creates a trust deficit that the leasing and management teams spend weeks recovering from.

Preventative maintenance is another major piece. Executing PM programs consistently is what keeps a property from sliding into reactive-only mode, which is expensive, exhausting, and bad for asset value. You'll also handle grounds upkeep, snow removal, and general safety and cleanliness standards. And yes, you're on the emergency on-call rotation. Nights and weekends happen in this work. That's not a footnote.

What You Bring

  • At least 5 years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • Solid hands-on skills across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • HVAC certification, EPA certification, and/or NAPE certification
  • Familiarity with OSHA standards, risk management principles, and MSDS
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting skills
  • Exposure to complex mechanical systems (central plant, chillers, boilers, split systems) is a plus
  • Supervisory or leadership experience, even informal, matters here
  • Comfort with technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office proficiency
  • Physical ability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently, climb ladders, and work in varying weather

The Bigger Picture

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this role isn't purely technical skill. Plenty of maintenance techs can fix a leak. The people who thrive at the assistant manager level are the ones who can communicate clearly with residents after something went wrong, coach a junior tech without micromanaging, and spot a small problem before it becomes a capex conversation. If you're the person on your current team who others quietly come to when something complicated breaks, that's a good sign this role fits you.

The salary range for this position is $65,000 to $70,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5195786008.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
greenhouse_5195786008.rewritten_description:
The Role in Plain Terms

This is a hands-on maintenance leadership position at a Bozzuto residential community in East Boston. You're not just fixing things. You're running the floor, guiding your team, and making sure the building stays in shape from the mechanical room to the grounds. The make-ready process, preventative maintenance, and emergency response all run through you.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

Your days split between technical work and team oversight. You're resolving HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance issues yourself when needed, but you're also checking that your team closes service requests completely and on time. Residents notice the follow-through. Sloppy ticket closure shows up in reviews and renewal rates.

Turn management is a core part of this job. When a unit goes vacant, you're coordinating the make-ready from punch list to final walk so leasing can put it back on the market without delays. A slow turn costs the property real money.

  • Execute and oversee preventative maintenance schedules for all building systems
  • Maintain compliance with applicable building codes, OSHA standards, and safety protocols
  • Handle general upkeep including grounds maintenance and snow removal
  • Participate in the emergency on-call rotation and weekend coverage
  • Work with complex mechanical systems including boilers, chillers, and central plant equipment

East Boston properties deal with older building stock and New England winters. If you've managed boiler systems and handled cold-weather grounds operations, that experience translates directly here.

What Bozzuto Expects You to Bring

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management practices
  • Hands-on proficiency across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and paint skills
  • Supervisory experience or a demonstrated ability to lead a small crew
  • Comfort with technology platforms and Microsoft Office basics
  • Physical capability to lift 25 to 50 pounds frequently and work in varying weather

The candidates who stand out aren't just technically sharp. They communicate clearly with residents, hold their team accountable without micromanaging, and treat the property like they own it. That "know it like you built it" mentality matters at Bozzuto because it shows in the condition of the asset over time.

Compensation and Growth

The salary range is $67,000 to $69,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

For someone with strong technical skills who's ready to move into full maintenance management, this role is a direct path. Bozzuto promotes internally and the company manages properties at scale across multiple markets. An Assistant Maintenance Manager who builds a clean record here, keeps NOI protected through solid preventative maintenance, and develops a reliable team has a clear route to a Maintenance Manager title.

The skills transfer more than people expect, particularly the customer-facing pressure and the ability to manage a team through unpredictable days.

greenhouse_5195970008.title: Assistant Property Manager
greenhouse_5195970008.rewritten_description:
It's a Tuesday afternoon. A prospective resident just walked in unannounced, a current resident is at the desk asking about a charge on their ledger, and the property manager is off site. You handle all three without breaking stride. That's the job, and if you're good at it, it's genuinely satisfying.

What This Role Actually Covers

Bozzuto is hiring an Assistant Property Manager for their Los Angeles community, and the scope here is real. You're not just covering leasing when traffic is slow. You're running point on delinquency, coaching leasing staff, conducting tours that convert, and stepping into the property manager seat whenever they're out. The financial side is part of your daily rhythm: rent collection, budget awareness, and understanding how your decisions affect the community's bottom line.

The role also has a visible, outward-facing component. Bozzuto expects their APMs to represent the brand crisply, keep the property's interior and exterior in sharp condition, and build genuine connections with residents. Social media content creation is part of the expectation too, specifically Instagram and Facebook, so comfort with those platforms matters beyond just personal use.

Weekend availability is required. One to two weekends per month, with compensating weekdays off. That's standard for a lease-up or high-traffic urban community, and it's worth knowing upfront rather than discovering later.

What Bozzuto Is Looking For

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience that demonstrates the same level of professional development
  • At least two years in property management, hospitality, or retail, where you've managed customer relationships and handled pressure gracefully
  • Exposure to P&L, budgets, or revenue management in some form
  • Proficiency with Yardi or similar property management software
  • Strong written and verbal communication, the kind that works equally well in a lease signing and a difficult resident conversation
  • A real sales instinct, not just a script

The salary range sits between $69,000 and $72,000, with bonus eligibility on top. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

From a career development standpoint, the APM role is one of the clearest paths into property management. The skills you build here, particularly the financial fluency and team leadership, translate directly into a property manager seat. APMs who develop strong NOI awareness and can manage a leasing team tend to move up faster than those who stay in pure leasing. Bozzuto operates across multiple markets and asset classes, which gives you exposure beyond a single community type. That breadth matters when you're ready for the next step.

greenhouse_5197331008.title: Assistant Property Manager
greenhouse_5197331008.rewritten_description:
Property management communities live or die by the quality of their mid-level leadership. The Assistant Property Manager role exists precisely because a single Property Manager can't be everywhere at once, and the gap between a well-run community and a struggling one often comes down to who's holding things together when that PM steps out. At Bozzuto's Wellington, FL community, this position carries real operational weight.

What This Role Actually Involves

Day to day, you're splitting your attention between the financial side of the property and the human side. On the numbers end, that means tracking delinquency, managing rent collection, and keeping a close eye on the community's financial performance. You'll have exposure to P&L and budget management, so this isn't a role where you just hand off billing questions to someone else.

On the people side, you're conducting tours, building genuine connections with prospective residents, and coaching the leasing team by example. You're also the one stepping into the Property Manager seat when they're out. That's not a ceremonial title. It means you're making real decisions about operations, resident concerns, and team direction.

Weekend availability is part of the deal. Bozzuto asks for one to two weekends per month, with compensating weekdays off. If you've worked in multifamily before, you already know that Saturday traffic is often your highest-converting leasing window. That's why it matters.

Yardi proficiency is preferred, and comfort with social media platforms like Instagram and Facebook is genuinely expected here. Not just "I have an account" comfortable. Bozzuto wants someone who can create content that reflects the community and actually connects with the local audience.

What Sets Strong Candidates Apart

The honest answer is this: candidates who treat delinquency management as a customer service exercise, not just a collections task, tend to perform better in APM roles. Residents who are late on rent are often dealing with real stress. How you handle that conversation affects both your recovery rate and your renewal rate. It takes a specific kind of emotional intelligence to hold the line while keeping the relationship intact.

A real estate license is required or must be obtained within 90 days of hire where applicable. If you're coming in without one, have a plan ready.

  • 4-year degree or equivalent hands-on experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • 2 or more years in a relevant industry role
  • Demonstrated exposure to budget and P&L responsibilities
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Proficiency with industry software, Yardi preferred
  • Comfort with social media content creation
  • Sales instincts and the ability to close without being pushy

The salary range for this position is $56,000 to $64,000, with bonus eligibility on top of base. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone looking to grow toward a Property Manager role, this is a legitimate stepping stone with a company that has the infrastructure to support that path.

greenhouse_5197383008.title: Assistant Property Manager
greenhouse_5197383008.rewritten_description:
It's a Tuesday afternoon and a prospective resident just walked in unannounced, the leasing consultant is on a tour, and the Property Manager is offsite. You're the one who steps up, pulls the right unit, delivers a compelling walkthrough, and closes the lease. That's a normal day in this role.

The Position

Bozzuto is hiring an Assistant Property Manager for their Washington, DC community (20037). What This Role Requires

greenhouse_5188156008.title: Assistant Property Manager
greenhouse_5188156008.rewritten_description:

When the Property Manager is out, you lead. That's not ceremonial. It means making decisions, managing the team, and being accountable for whatever comes up that day. You'll also contribute to social media presence on platforms like Facebook and Instagram, coach team members, and make sure the community's physical presentation stays sharp inside and out.

Weekend availability is required, roughly one to two weekends per month, with corresponding weekdays off. That's the standard rhythm in residential property management, and it's worth knowing upfront.

greenhouse_5192108008.title: Evening Concierge
greenhouse_5192108008.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This is the 3PM to 11PM shift, Monday through Friday, at a Bozzuto community in Miami. The evening hours matter here. You're not handling leasing appointments or morning move-ins. You're the person residents see when they get home from work, tired, carrying groceries, maybe frustrated. Your job is to make that moment better every single time.

Concretely, that means running the front desk, managing package intake (volume spikes hard during the holidays), coordinating club room and freight elevator reservations, and keeping every log tight and current. It also means planning and executing resident events that actually build community rather than just filling a calendar. You'll manage the community's social media presence, push for positive resident reviews, and respond to whatever comes through the door with a calm, professional demeanor. In a Miami high-rise, "whatever comes through the door" can include a lot.

Bilingual English/Spanish is strongly preferred here. Miami's resident base is genuinely diverse, and the ability to switch languages mid-conversation isn't a nice-to-have in this market. It's often the difference between a resident feeling heard and a resident feeling dismissed.

What You Bring

  • At least 2 years in hospitality, hotel, retail, or travel. Front desk hotel experience translates especially well to this role.
  • Bilingual English/Spanish, strongly preferred
  • Strong written and verbal communication, including the ability to represent the community professionally on social media
  • Comfort with software, apps, and property management platforms
  • A sharp, polished appearance and genuine attention to detail
  • Physical ability to lift up to 25 pounds. Package delivery is a real part of the job.

The Bigger Picture

Resident satisfaction scores and online reviews directly influence a community's ability to maintain occupancy and reduce concessions. A concierge who builds real relationships with residents, knows their names, knows their dogs, and handles problems before they escalate, shows up in the numbers. Lower delinquency starts with residents who feel respected. Renewals happen when people feel at home.

This role pays between $45,000 and $47,000 annually, with additional bonus opportunities. Bozzuto's benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone building a career in luxury property operations, a concierge role at a Bozzuto community is a real credential. The service standards are high, the training is structured, and the exposure to how a well-run community operates gives you a foundation that transfers directly into leasing, assistant management, or community management over time.

greenhouse_5188043008.title: Evening Concierge
greenhouse_5188043008.rewritten_description:

It's the connective tissue between a resident's daily life and the experience the property promises to deliver. Bozzuto, one of the more recognized names in upscale property management along the East Coast, builds communities where that experience is the product.

You're catching residents as they return from work, which means you're fielding a concentrated burst of energy, requests, package pickups, and casual conversation every single evening. It's not a slow desk job. It's relationship management at a consistent, predictable pace.

What the Role Actually Involves

You'll own the front desk experience during the hours when the building is most alive. That means greeting residents by name (and yes, knowing their dogs too), managing package intake and retrieval, keeping logs and reservations organized, and handling whatever comes up with composure. Bozzuto also expects this role to contribute to community culture through resident events and social media presence, so there's a creative dimension here that goes beyond standard concierge work.

  • Two or more years in hospitality, hotel, retail, or travel environments
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and digital tools

  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds for package handling

The salary for this role is $48,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of PTO plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Where This Role Leads

People underestimate concierge experience as a launchpad in property management. What you're actually building here is a fluency in resident relations, community operations, and brand representation that transfers directly into leasing roles. Concierges who develop sharp instincts for what residents need and what makes them stay often move into Leasing Consultant or Assistant Community Manager positions, sometimes at the same property. The interpersonal skills you sharpen here, knowing how to read a frustrated resident, how to de-escalate, how to make someone feel genuinely welcomed, are exactly what separates strong leasing candidates from average ones. If you have a hospitality background and you're curious about where a career in property management could go, this is a thoughtful entry point with a company that has the infrastructure to grow you.

greenhouse_5173290008.title: Evening Concierge
greenhouse_5173290008.rewritten_description:

greenhouse_5178302008.title: Evening Concierge (Mon - Fri, 3-11PM)
greenhouse_5178302008.rewritten_description:
Skills That Drive This Role

This position runs on interpersonal intelligence. The Evening Concierge role at Bozzuto's Washington, DC property asks you to read people quickly, adjust your tone and approach on the fly, and make every resident feel like the building knows them personally. You'll also use organizational skills constantly. Package volume at a DC high-rise or mid-rise can get intense, especially around the holidays. Keeping the package room accurate, managing freight elevator reservations, maintaining front desk logs, and tracking club room bookings all happen in parallel with greeting residents coming off a long commute. Social media fluency matters here too. Bozzuto uses concierge staff to help generate authentic community content and encourage residents to post reviews. greenhouse_5161922008.rewritten_description:

greenhouse_5165298008.title: Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_5165298008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation in multifamily that most operators spend decades chasing. That means following up on leads quickly and with substance, running personalized tours that actually move people toward a decision, and staying engaged with prospective residents throughout the process. The schedule runs three weekends per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's the honest trade-off in leasing, and it's worth knowing upfront. Saturday and Sunday are when foot traffic peaks. Serious candidates understand this and plan around it rather than resist it.

Weekends are part of the schedule. Three per month, specifically, with compensating weekdays off. That's the rhythm of leasing and it's worth knowing going in.

Beyond tours, you'll manage social media presence on platforms like Instagram and Facebook, creating content that builds awareness and keeps the community visible to people who haven't started their formal search yet. It's a mix of relationship work and marketing instinct that not every leasing role asks for, but Bozzuto treats it as core to the job.

Compensation sits between $50,000 and $52,000 annually, with bonus opportunities on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Who Fits This Role Well

  • Strong written and verbal communicator who adapts tone depending on the audience
  • Comfortable with CRM tools, leasing software, and social platforms without needing hand-holding
  • Professionally polished in appearance and in how they handle difficult or indecisive prospects
  • Genuinely interested in people, not just closing a lease
  • Sales-minded enough to follow up consistently without being pushy

What separates strong leasing consultants from average ones here isn't just closing ability. It's the follow-through after the tour: the timely callback, the accurate answer to a lease question, the social post that actually reflects the community's personality. Bozzuto communities tend to attract residents who have options, so the experience you deliver has to hold up against competitors who are also offering concessions and move-in specials. Your instincts about people matter as much as your process.

For someone looking at a longer career path, this role builds a foundation in sales, resident relations, and marketing that translates directly into Assistant Manager and Property Manager roles over time. The skills you develop here, reading prospects, understanding traffic patterns, connecting resident experience to occupancy, are the same ones that drive performance at every level of property management.

That's the job Bozzuto is filling here.

What You're Actually Running

This is a full-scope maintenance leadership role at a Bozzuto-managed residential community. You're not wrenching alone. You're building a team, setting the pace, and owning every system on the asset. HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliances, central plant exposure if applicable. You'll manage the turn process from punch list to move-in ready, and you'll be the person who makes sure that process doesn't create leasing friction when the community needs to fill units. Preventative maintenance programs, vendor contracts, capital improvement projects, and 24/7 emergency coverage all fall within your scope.

Day to day, you're balancing incoming service tickets against scheduled PM work, staffing the rotation, and making sure the building looks and functions the way it's supposed to. You'll also carry direct responsibility for code compliance, annual systems inspections, and keeping the operation accident-free. The safety mindset here isn't a soft ask. It's a hard requirement.

What Bozzuto Needs From You

  • 7 or more years in facilities or residential building maintenance, with genuine depth in HVAC, plumbing, and electrical
  • At least 3 years leading a maintenance team, including hiring, developing, and retaining techs
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Exposure to complex mechanical systems: central plant, boilers, chillers, split systems
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, risk management, and MSDS requirements
  • Basic carpentry, drywall, and paint skills to close out turns without waiting on contractors
  • Comfort with technology platforms, work order software, and Microsoft Office
  • Availability for on-call emergency rotation

The "builder's mentality" language in the posting is worth taking seriously. Bozzuto wants someone who knows the property systems well enough to catch problems before they become work orders, not someone who reacts after something breaks. That depth of ownership is what separates a strong candidate from a technically capable one who still needs managing.

Oakland's housing market means residents have options. A maintenance operation that runs clean, turns units fast, and responds to service requests quickly is a real retention factor. Your work has a direct line to resident satisfaction scores and, by extension, to renewal rates. That's the operational reality of this role, and it's worth knowing before you step into it.

Bozzuto offers competitive compensation with bonus eligibility, medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement for ongoing career development.

greenhouse_5147772008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation across the mid-Atlantic and Southeast for operating well-run communities where the physical product and the resident experience are both taken seriously. This Maintenance Technician opening in Atlanta's 30309 corridor puts you inside that operation at the ground level, which is exactly where durable technical skills get built.

The work itself covers the full range of what a capable technician handles daily: service ticket resolution, HVAC troubleshooting, plumbing and electrical repairs, appliance work, and turns. Make-readies matter here. A vacant unit sitting unready costs the property real money, and Bozzuto treats that process with the same attention they give occupied-unit service. You'll also carry a share of the on-call rotation, which means after-hours emergencies are part of the deal. That's honest, and it's worth knowing upfront.

The role is open to candidates who are earlier in their career. Beginner to intermediate HVAC proficiency is the stated bar, and that's meaningful. Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first year on the job. For someone without that credential, this is a real path to earning it at no cost, and the 608 Universal opens doors across every class of residential property you might work at later.

What the role requires

  • Some background in building maintenance or formal trades education
  • Working knowledge of HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair (beginner to intermediate)
  • Availability for an on-call rotation and occasional weekend or evening shifts
  • Reliable personal transportation for commuting and emergency response
  • Ability to regularly move and carry 25 to 50 pounds
  • Basic comfort with software and mobile apps used for work orders and communication
  • Strong written and verbal communication, a professional appearance, and genuine service orientation

Compensation sits between $49,000 and $51,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Here's where this role leads if you're thinking a few steps ahead. Technicians who develop strong HVAC and electrical depth, learn to manage their own work order queues efficiently, and build real relationships with residents tend to move into Lead Technician or Service Manager roles within two to four years. Those positions carry meaningful responsibility over a property's maintenance budget and vendor relationships, which is the kind of operational experience that eventually supports a move into Facilities Management or even Property Management if that's the direction you want. The technical credibility you build now compounds. It's not just a job with a wrench.

greenhouse_5153818008.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5153818008.rewritten_description:
Maintenance Technician roles at mid-rise and high-rise communities in San Francisco demand a specific kind of person: someone who can handle a packed ticket queue in the morning, troubleshoot a tricky HVAC issue by lunch, and still bring genuine patience to a resident conversation at the end of the day. Bozzuto is hiring for exactly that at their San Francisco property.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Most of your time will split between service ticket resolution and keeping vacant units move-in ready. Turns are a real part of this job. You're walking punch lists, completing make-ready repairs, and making sure each unit is clean, functional, and up to standard before a new resident walks through the door. Beyond turns, you'll handle the full range: HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair. Beginner to intermediate proficiency across those trades is where Bozzuto expects you to start, not where they expect you to finish.

There's also a grounds and building upkeep component. Exterior cleanliness, safety walkthroughs, and preventative maintenance are part of the rhythm here, not afterthoughts. And yes, this role includes an on-call rotation. After-hours emergencies are real, and you'll need reliable personal transportation to respond when they come up. That's worth knowing before you apply, not after.

What You're Bringing

  • Hands-on experience in building maintenance or a trade education background
  • Working knowledge of HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair (beginner to intermediate)
  • Availability for weekends, evenings, and on-call emergency response
  • The physical capacity to lift and move 25 to 50 pounds regularly
  • Basic computer skills and comfort using maintenance software or apps
  • Strong written and verbal communication, and a professional presence on site
  • Reliable personal transportation

Growth and What Bozzuto Brings Back

One concrete perk worth highlighting: after your 12-month anniversary, Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. That's a credential that travels with you for the rest of your career in property maintenance, and they're covering the cost entirely. The salary range for this position sits between $54,110 and $58,000, with additional bonus opportunities on top. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

For someone who's solid in the trades but still building out their certifications and experience, this is a reasonable place to do it. Technicians who develop strong diagnostic skills and consistent communication habits tend to move toward Lead Technician or Service Manager roles over time. Bozzuto operates across multiple markets, which means internal mobility is a real path for people who perform well.

The job isn't glamorous. It's early mornings, physical work, occasional late-night calls, and a lot of problem-solving under pressure. But if you're the kind of person who takes quiet satisfaction in fixing things right the first time and leaving a unit better than you found it, this role has a clear structure and a company behind it that invests in the people doing the actual work.

greenhouse_5154557008.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5154557008.rewritten_description:
Where Hands-On Skills Meet Real Career Momentum

Maintenance work at a well-run residential community is one of the best skill-building environments in property management. You're touching HVAC systems, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair, and make-readies all in the same week. That breadth is rare in most trades jobs, and it compounds fast. Bozzuto is hiring a Maintenance Technician at their Durham, NC community, and the role is structured in a way that rewards people who want to keep learning.

The day-to-day centers on service ticket resolution and preventative maintenance, which means you're not just reacting to problems. You're developing the pattern recognition that separates a technician who fixes things from one who understands why things break. That distinction matters a lot in this industry. Residents notice the difference too, and Bozzuto takes resident experience seriously at every level of their operation.

What This Role Actually Exercises

You'll need beginner to intermediate proficiency across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. You don't need to be an expert in all of them on day one, but you do need enough foundation to work independently and grow. The turn process is a consistent part of the job. Preparing vacant units for move-in, working through punch list items, and getting apartments rent-ready on time are skills that translate directly into lead technician and service manager roles down the road.

  • Diagnosing and resolving HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance issues
  • Completing make-ready turns and unit improvements between residents
  • Contributing to grounds upkeep, building cleanliness, and snow removal
  • Participating in an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies
  • Communicating clearly with residents and teammates through service requests

The on-call rotation is worth naming honestly. It's part of the role, and it requires reliable personal transportation and the flexibility to respond when things go sideways at 10pm. That's standard in residential maintenance, and it's also exactly the kind of accountability that gets noticed when advancement conversations happen.

What Bozzuto Adds to the Equation

One thing that stands out here: Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first 12 months. That's a meaningful credential for any technician building toward a service manager or facilities role, and the fact that they fund it says something about how they view technician development. Most operators talk about growth; covering certification costs is a concrete version of that commitment.

The salary range runs from $46,000 to $48,500, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Bozzuto has a national footprint and a strong reputation in the mid-Atlantic and Southeast markets, which means the skills you build here carry weight if you ever want to move up internally or into a different company later. Strong technicians who can communicate well and show up consistently tend to move into lead tech or service manager positions within two to three years in environments like this one.

If you're coming from a trade background or a maintenance role and want a place where the work is varied, the training is real, and the path forward is visible, this is worth a close look.

greenhouse_5131226008.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5131226008.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This is a live-on-site maintenance technician role at a Bozzuto-managed property in Mt. Vernon, NY. That last part matters. Living on-site means you're the first call when something goes wrong at 11pm on a Tuesday. A toilet won't stop running. A resident locked themselves out. A heating unit stops working in February. You'll handle it. That's the honest reality of the position, and it's worth knowing upfront.

Day to day, you're working through service tickets across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. You're also doing turns on vacant units, which means getting apartments cleaned up, patched, and move-in ready between residents. Punch list items, minor repairs, general upkeep. You'll contribute to grounds maintenance and snow removal, which in Westchester County winters is a real part of the job, not a footnote.

Bozzuto will pay for your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification after your first 12 months. That's a concrete, marketable credential that opens doors in commercial and residential maintenance work. It's not a small thing.

What You Bring

  • Some background in building maintenance, whether through hands-on experience or trade education
  • Beginner to intermediate skills across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair
  • Reliable personal transportation for getting to work and responding to after-hours calls
  • Physical capability to lift and move 25 to 50 pounds regularly, sometimes more
  • Comfort using basic software, apps, and work order platforms
  • Clear written and verbal communication skills
  • Willingness to work weekends, evenings, and participate in an on-call rotation
  • Ability to live on-site at the property (required for this role)

The Honest Context

On-site maintenance roles attract two kinds of candidates. The first type sees the on-call requirement as a dealbreaker. The second type recognizes that living on-site often comes with real compensation advantages and that being embedded in the property builds skills faster than almost any other setup in residential maintenance. If you're the second type, this role fits well.

The salary range is $54,000 to $56,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Bozzuto's benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401(k) with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The company has a genuine reputation for internal development, so this isn't necessarily a ceiling. Technicians who build their skills and earn certifications often move into lead technician or service manager roles over time.

If you want a position where you're learning real trades work, building credentials the industry respects, and you're not afraid of the accountability that comes with living where you work, this one is worth a serious look.

greenhouse_5163544008.title: Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5163544008.rewritten_description:
Maintenance work is a trade skill job first. The soft skills matter, but if you can't clear a drain, swap out a breaker, or diagnose why a condenser isn't cooling, the rest of it doesn't matter. This role at Bozzuto's Watertown property leans on hands-on technical ability across the full range of residential systems: HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. Beginner to intermediate proficiency is the stated floor, and that's honest. What you're actually being hired for is a combination of technical competence, reliability, and a genuine willingness to serve people who live in your building.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

A good portion of your week runs on service ticket volume. Residents submit requests, you resolve them. Some are simple. Some are not. Between those, you're doing preventative maintenance work that keeps the bigger problems from showing up at 11pm on a Friday. And they still will sometimes, because that's the job. On-call rotation is real here, and you'll need your own transportation to respond when it's your turn.

Turn work is the other major thread. When a unit goes vacant, you're doing the make-ready: repairs, touch-ups, punch list items that have to be closed before the next resident moves in. The speed and quality of that work directly affects how quickly a unit can be re-leased, so it's not background noise. It connects to the broader operation in a concrete way.

  • HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair (beginner to intermediate proficiency required)
  • Service ticket resolution and preventative maintenance programs
  • Unit turns and make-ready preparation
  • Grounds upkeep, snow removal, and general building cleanliness
  • Weekend and evening availability, including on-call emergency response
  • Physical demands include frequently moving 25 to 50 pounds
  • Basic computer skills for work order and maintenance software

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

We've seen technicians who are excellent with tools but treat residents like interruptions. And we've seen people with great attitudes who can't close a work order without three follow-ups. The ones who do well in this kind of role carry both: real technical range across systems, and the communication habits that keep residents from escalating every small issue into a complaint. Written and verbal communication skills are listed for a reason. A lot of resident frustration comes from not knowing what's happening, not from the repair itself taking time.

Bozzuto offers something worth noting for someone earlier in their career: after your first 12 months, they'll cover the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. That's a credential that travels with you regardless of where your career goes. For a technician looking to move toward lead or service manager roles over time, that certification plus hands-on HVAC experience at a well-regarded management company is a real foundation.

The Bozzuto Context

Bozzuto operates across development, construction, and management, which means the properties they manage tend to be well-maintained and the expectations for presentation are high. Working at one of their communities in Watertown puts you in a market where Class A resident expectations are standard. The company has a genuine reputation for culture and internal development, and the benefits package here, including medical, dental, vision, 20 days PTO, 401k with match, and tuition reimbursement, reflects that. This isn't a cut-rate operation.
