You'll be expected to create and post content on platforms like Instagram and Facebook to generate awareness and keep the communities top of mind for people who are still in research mode. It's not just liking posts; it's original content that actually reflects the character of the property.

The schedule runs on a weekend-heavy rhythm. Three weekends per month with two weekdays off is the structure. If you've worked leasing before, you know weekends are when serious prospects show up. If this is your first leasing role, adjust your expectations early and you'll be fine.

What You Bring and Where It Takes You

  • Strong written and verbal communication, you're comfortable on the phone, in person, and in a caption
  • A genuine interest in helping people find a place to live, not just closing for the sake of it
  • Comfort with CRM tools, property management software, and social platforms
  • A professional presentation and the kind of energy that puts a hesitant prospect at ease
  • Sales instincts, meaning you can read where someone is in their decision and respond to that, not just recite features

The salary range for this role sits between $51,500 and $55,000, with additional bonus opportunities tied to performance. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Here's the career angle worth considering. Multi-site experience compounds faster than single-property experience. You see more traffic, more objection types, more closing scenarios, and you learn how different communities position themselves against each other. Leasing consultants who've worked across multiple assets tend to move into Assistant Manager or Leasing Manager roles with a stronger operational foundation than those who've only worked one door. At a company like Bozzuto, which manages a significant portfolio across the mid-Atlantic and Northeast, internal mobility is a real path, not just a recruiting line.

greenhouse_5170383008.title: Multi-Site Maintenance Manager
greenhouse_5170383008.rewritten_description:
Picture this: it's 7 a.m. on a Tuesday, and you're walking two properties before your techs clock in. You're checking mechanical rooms, logging what needs attention, and mentally prioritizing the punch list for three turns scheduled to deliver by Friday. That's the rhythm of this role.

What This Role Actually Covers

Bozzuto is hiring a Multi-Site Maintenance Manager based in Santa Monica to lead maintenance operations across multiple residential communities. This isn't a wrench-turning position. It's a leadership role where your technical depth earns you credibility, but your ability to build and develop a team determines how well the properties actually run.

You'll own the full make-ready process, ensuring turns move efficiently so units can be leased without delay. Preventative maintenance programs, annual building systems inspections, capital improvement projects, and vendor management all fall under your scope. You're also responsible for keeping the operation compliant with applicable building, county, and safety codes, and running an accident-free shop. The on-call rotation is real. Emergency coverage is 24/7, and you'll need to be available when situations arise.

On the technical side, Bozzuto expects advanced proficiency in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair. Exposure to complex mechanical systems including central plant, boilers, chillers, and split systems is a genuine requirement here, not a nice-to-have. Basic carpentry, drywall, and painting round out what you're expected to handle or confidently supervise.

What You'll Need to Bring

  • 7 or more years in facilities or residential building maintenance, with a trades education background
  • At least 3 years of supervisory experience, including hiring, developing, and retaining maintenance staff
  • HVAC, EPA, and/or NAPE certification
  • Working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management practices
  • Comfort with technology platforms, Microsoft Office, and property management apps
  • Physical capability to frequently handle 25 to 50 pounds of force, climb ladders, and work in varying weather conditions

The salary range runs from $82,000 to $100,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Bozzuto's benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in a multi-site role like this is the ability to triage across locations without letting any single property drift. The managers who thrive here build systems, not dependency. They train their techs to handle the routine so they can focus on the complex, the capital projects, and the vendor relationships that protect NOI long-term. If you've been running a single property and you're ready to scale your impact, this is a logical and meaningful step up.

greenhouse_5188583008.title: Multi-Site Maintenance Technician ($2,500 Sign-On Bonus)
greenhouse_5188583008.rewritten_description:
It's Tuesday morning and you're pulling into Atlantic Station before most residents have had their coffee. There's a work order for a leaking fixture in one unit, a vacant apartment that needs punch list items cleared before a Friday move-in, and a heads-up from the overnight on-call log about a heating complaint. That's a pretty normal start to the day in this role.

What This Role Actually Involves

Bozzuto is hiring a Multi-Site Maintenance Technician to support two connected properties: Atlantic Station and Atlantic Station West in Stamford, CT. You'll split your time across both sites handling service ticket resolution, preventative maintenance, and the turn-over work that gets vacant units ready for incoming residents. The core technical work spans HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and appliance repair at a beginner-to-intermediate level. You don't need to be a master of every trade, but you do need a genuine foundation and the drive to build on it.

Beyond the mechanical work, you're also part of how residents experience the community. Grounds upkeep, snow removal, common area cleanliness, and showing up professionally for every interaction all matter here. Weekend availability and participation in an on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies are part of the deal. That's worth knowing upfront. On-call is real, and reliable personal transportation is a hard requirement for responding when something comes up outside business hours.

The salary range for this position is $47,000 to $50,000, and there's a $2,500 sign-on bonus attached. Additional bonus opportunities exist beyond that.

What You Bring

  • Hands-on experience in building or residential maintenance, or a trades education background
  • Working knowledge of HVAC, plumbing, electrical systems, and appliance repair (beginner to intermediate)
  • Reliable personal transportation for commuting and on-call response
  • Physical capability to frequently lift and move 25 to 50 pounds
  • Comfort using software, apps, and basic computer programs for work order management
  • Strong communication skills and a professional, service-oriented approach

One thing that separates strong candidates in this kind of multi-site role: the ability to self-manage your time across two properties without needing someone to tell you where the priority is. When you're splitting days between Atlantic Station and Atlantic Station West, you'll regularly have to triage competing demands. Technicians who thrive here treat the work order queue like a living document, not a to-do list they'll get to eventually.

Bozzuto also offers a tangible growth incentive for this position. After your one-year anniversary, the company will cover the cost of your EPA 608 Universal Refrigerant Certification. That's a real credential that opens doors to higher-level HVAC work and carries weight across the industry. Combined with tuition reimbursement and a 401k with company match, the benefits package reflects a company that's thinking beyond just filling the role.

greenhouse_5198850008.title: Overnight Concierge
greenhouse_5198850008.rewritten_description:
Luxury residential properties at the mid-rise and high-rise level live and die by the quality of their resident experience. The overnight shift is where that experience either holds or falls apart. Bozzuto's Overnight Concierge role at their Reston, VA community exists precisely because residents coming home at midnight deserve the same level of attention as those walking in at noon.

This is a Monday through Friday position, 11pm to 7am. That detail matters. It's a consistent schedule, which is genuinely rare in hospitality-adjacent roles, and it means you're owning the overnight hours for a community that expects a lot from its building staff. You're the face people see when they get home from a late flight or a long shift. You're handling package management (which gets serious during the holidays), coordinating freight elevator reservations, keeping front desk logs clean, and managing clubroom bookings. The administrative side is real and it requires someone who finds satisfaction in precision, not just in conversation.

What the Day-to-Day Actually Looks Like

Overnight concierge work has a rhythm that's different from daytime front desk roles. Traffic is lighter but the interactions are more personal. Residents who come home late tend to remember who greeted them by name, who already knew their dog's name, and who quietly solved a problem without making them feel like a burden. You're also expected to plan and execute resident events, manage the community's social media presence, and encourage satisfied residents to leave reviews. That's a broader scope than most overnight roles carry, and it's worth noting if you're comparing opportunities.

Bozzuto is looking for someone with at least two years in hospitality, retail, travel, or hotel environments. Strong written and verbal communication skills matter here, as does comfort with software and apps used for building operations. The ability to lift up to 25 pounds rounds out the physical requirements.

Where This Role Can Take You

Concierge experience at a company like Bozzuto is a real foundation. The skills you build here, reading residents, managing competing priorities calmly, keeping a community feeling cohesive, transfer directly into leasing, assistant management, and eventually community management. Many strong leasing consultants and assistant managers came up through concierge and front desk roles because they already understood how residents think and what they actually value. If you want to move into property operations long-term, this is a legitimate on-ramp, not a dead end.

  • 2+ years in hospitality, hotel, retail, or travel
  • Strong written and verbal communication
  • Comfort with software, apps, and computer programs
  • Detail-oriented with an instinct for anticipating needs
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds
  • Professional appearance and customer-first mindset

The salary for this position is $48,000 annually, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5191065008.title: Package Handler (Sunday-Thursday, 11:30a - 7:30p)
greenhouse_5191065008.rewritten_description:
What You'll Actually Do

This role is simpler to describe than it is to execute well. You're receiving, logging, and delivering packages to residents at a Bozzuto community in Roselle, NJ, working a Sunday through Thursday shift from 11:30am to 7:30pm. That schedule matters. You're covering the window when package volume peaks and residents are home expecting their deliveries.

The physical side is real. You'll spend most of your shift on your feet, continuously lifting and carrying packages that commonly weigh anywhere from 25 to 75 pounds. Package rooms don't organize themselves, and an audit trail that's even one day behind creates problems fast. Every package gets logged, tracked, and delivered within 24 hours of receipt. That's the standard.

The resident-facing piece is where this role separates itself from a warehouse job. You're often the only Bozzuto team member a resident sees that day. A quick, genuine exchange when handing off a package does more for resident retention than most people expect. The residents who feel known and cared for are the ones who renew.

What You Bring

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Reliable personal transportation to get yourself to the property consistently
  • Physical stamina for continuous lifting, lowering, and carrying throughout your shift
  • Comfort working independently without constant supervision while still hitting daily deadlines
  • A calm, professional manner with residents, even when they're frustrated about a missing or delayed package

Context Worth Knowing

Package volume at apartment communities has grown dramatically over the past several years and shows no sign of slowing. Many Class A and B properties now treat package management as a dedicated operational function rather than a task handed off to whoever's available at the front desk. Filling that role well means understanding that you're part of the service infrastructure, not separate from it.

Skills you build here transfer directly into leasing and resident services roles. You develop a feel for resident communication, you learn how a well-run community handles daily operations, and you build a track record with a company that promotes from within. Bozzuto is a recognized name in the mid-Atlantic property management space, and starting on the operations side gives you a grounded view of how a community actually functions day to day.

Compensation for this position falls between $50,000 and $53,000 annually, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5182481008.title: Part-Time Resident Experience Associate (Wednesday - Friday, 12pm - 8pm)
greenhouse_5182481008.rewritten_description:
What This Role Actually Is

Bozzuto runs some of the more polished communities in the mid-Atlantic and Southeast, and their leasing and resident experience teams reflect that. This part-time Associate position covers Wednesday through Friday, noon to eight, which means you're carrying the afternoon and evening shift when foot traffic picks up, packages pile in, and residents are coming home from work with questions, complaints, or both. It's a front-desk-forward role with a genuine leasing component layered in.

The Day-to-Day

You're the first face people see. That sounds simple, but at a Bozzuto community it carries real weight. You'll follow up with prospects, convert those conversations into tours, and hand warm leads off to the leasing team. Occupancy goals don't happen on their own, and this role contributes directly to them.

Resident retention is the other half of the job. You'll plan and run community events, keep the clubroom and package room organized, and manage front desk logistics with the kind of obsessive attention to detail that prevents small problems from becoming big ones. Social media falls in your lap too, including nudging satisfied residents toward leaving reviews. Beyond that, you're covering for teammates, anticipating needs before residents voice them, and generally keeping the community looking and running like it should.

A few things Bozzuto specifically looks for:

  • At least two years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or travel (customer-facing experience that's trained you to read a room)
  • Strong written and verbal communication
  • Comfort with software, apps, and property management platforms
  • A detail-oriented, anticipatory mindset
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds. Package volume during the holidays is real.

What Separates Strong Candidates Here

We've seen a lot of people apply to resident experience roles who treat them as glorified receptionist positions. The ones who thrive treat every prospect interaction as a leasing opportunity and every resident interaction as a retention play. Remembering names matters. Learning a resident's dog's name matters more than people expect. The evening shift specifically rewards candidates who stay sharp and personable at 7pm on a Friday when the rest of the office has been gone for hours.

This role pays $24 to $26 per hour. Bozzuto offers medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For a part-time schedule, that's a benefits package worth taking seriously. Career-wise, Resident Experience Associates at companies like Bozzuto often move into full-time leasing consultant roles or assistant manager tracks, especially once they have a few lease conversions and a retention event or two under their belt.

The Chapel Hill market is active, university-influenced, and competitive. Residents have options. The communities that win on retention are the ones where the front desk team genuinely knows who lives there.

greenhouse_5196899008.title: Porter
greenhouse_5196899008.rewritten_description:
Porter roles at well-run multifamily communities are more physical than people expect and more important than they get credit for. The condition of your grounds and common areas is often the first thing a prospective resident notices during a tour, and it's usually the last thing a long-term resident mentions when they renew. Bozzuto understands that, which is part of why they call this position a Community Care Associate. It's not just a title change.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

This is a grounds and common area role at a Bozzuto community in Aberdeen, MD. Day to day, you're keeping the property looking sharp: trash pickup, weed pulling, raking, watering, sweeping hallways and curbs, and checking that hall lights are working. Weekly tasks include cleaning handrails and vacuuming the pool area. Monthly and seasonal work rounds it out: hosing out hallways, power washing stairwells twice a year, and snow removal when Maryland decides to cooperate.

You'll also support the maintenance team with various tasks and painting as needed. That's worth noting because it's a real opportunity to pick up skills. Porters who pay attention during those assist jobs tend to move into maintenance tech roles faster than people realize.

  • Daily trash pickup and grounds upkeep
  • Weekly hallway sweeping, light checks, and handrail cleaning
  • Monthly hallway hosing and pool area vacuuming
  • Semi-annual stairwell power washing
  • Snow removal support as needed
  • Clubhouse and common area cleaning
  • Flier and newsletter delivery by 3:00 p.m. when requested
  • Maintenance and painting assistance

What You Need to Bring

Previous cleaning experience in multifamily or commercial settings is preferred. You need reliable personal transportation to get to the property. Physically, this role involves lifting up to 50 pounds and regular pushing, pulling, and climbing. That's not a formality. You're on your feet and moving throughout the shift.

One thing that separates solid porters from average ones: attention to detail without being told twice. Checking bandit signs daily, catching a burned-out hall light before a resident complains, noticing that a stairwell drain is clogged before it becomes a maintenance ticket. That kind of proactive eye is what property managers remember when a maintenance tech position opens up.

The Bozzuto Side of Things

Bozzuto has a real reputation in the mid-Atlantic multifamily market. They manage and develop quality communities, and they tend to invest in their people. This position comes with medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. The salary range is $42,024 to $43,000, with additional bonus opportunities available. For a porter role with a company of this caliber, that's a fair package. The path forward is there if you want it.

greenhouse_5147616008.title: Property Manager
greenhouse_5147616008.rewritten_description:
Property management at the senior level is a craft that blends financial discipline, team leadership, and resident experience into one daily practice. This Property Manager role at Bozzuto in Washington, DC puts all three of those skills to work simultaneously, and it rewards people who are genuinely strong across all of them, not just one or two.

Skills This Role Demands From Day One

You'll need a real command of P&L management. Bozzuto expects you to own the financial performance of the property, which means reading and interpreting operating statements, managing to budget, and producing accurate financial and operational reports for clients. If you've managed NOI targets and understand the relationship between occupancy, concessions, and net revenue, that foundation matters here.

On the leadership side, this isn't a role where you manage from a distance. Bozzuto is explicit that no task is below you. You're expected to step in, assist your team, and model the behaviors you want to see. That kind of hands-on leadership style is something you either already practice or you don't. It's hard to fake at this level.

Strong competitive market awareness is also a genuine requirement. Washington, DC is a dense, competitive rental market with constant new inventory coming online across neighborhoods like NoMa, Navy Yard, and Capitol Riverfront. Knowing how to read traffic patterns, adjust positioning, and advise on marketing strategy isn't optional. It's part of running the asset.

  • 5 or more years of experience in property management, hospitality, or retail leadership
  • Four-year degree or equivalent hands-on experience at a comparable level
  • Demonstrated track record managing revenue, budgets, and client relationships
  • Proficiency with Yardi or similar property management software
  • DC Property Manager license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills

What This Role Builds

Managing a Bozzuto community gives you exposure to third-party client reporting, which is a skill set that transfers directly into regional or portfolio-level roles. You're not just running an asset for an owner-operator. You're delivering financial transparency and performance accountability to a client, and that experience reads differently on a resume than straight in-house management.

You'll also develop your team coaching ability in a structured way. Bozzuto invests in professional development, and as the property manager, you're expected to drive that growth for your direct reports. People who sharpen this skill here often move into multi-site or regional manager roles with a clearer sense of how to build teams, not just manage them.

What Distinguishes Strong Candidates Here

The schedule includes one weekend per month with two weekdays off in exchange. That's a reasonable trade, but candidates who struggle with weekend availability tend to find it more disruptive than expected. Strong applicants come in clear-eyed about it.

Beyond schedule fit, the candidates who stand out are those who can hold a financial conversation with a client and then turn around and motivate a leasing associate in the same afternoon. The range of what this role requires in a single day is wide. Bozzuto's culture leans into that range rather than narrowing it, so the people who thrive here tend to find that variety energizing rather than exhausting.

The salary range for this position runs from $97,000 to $107,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5188377008.title: Property Manager
greenhouse_5188377008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation in multifamily that carries real weight. The company designs, builds, manages, and maintains properties with a level of operational discipline that most operators talk about but don't actually practice. This Property Manager role in Miami's 33127 zip code puts you at the center of that standard, running a community where the bar for resident experience and financial performance is genuinely high.

The job requires you to bring financial fluency on day one. You'll own P&L management, produce accurate reports for ownership clients, and track the property's performance against budget with enough detail to explain variances and propose corrections. Yardi proficiency is preferred, and a working knowledge of accounting principles isn't optional. If you've managed a T-12, presented financials to a client, and felt comfortable doing it, that's the baseline this role expects.

Sales and marketing are equally central. You're responsible for occupancy, and that means you understand the competitive set, you know what concessions the market is running, and you partner with marketing to execute strategies that convert traffic into signed leases and retain residents through renewal. Miami's rental market is dense and competitive, with strong demand but also constant pressure from new lease-up communities. Knowing your submarket cold isn't a bonus skill here. It's the job.

Leadership is the third pillar. You'll develop your team, hold them to Bozzuto's standards, and step in wherever the work needs doing. One weekend per month you'll be on-site when prospective residents are actively searching, which is the right call operationally even if it requires planning around your schedule.

What this role requires vs. what it builds

You need to arrive with at least five years of experience in property management, hospitality, or retail, along with a demonstrated track record in client relationships and team leadership. A four-year degree or equivalent experience rounds out the baseline. A real estate license is either required or something you'll need to obtain within 90 days of hire depending on applicable state rules.

What the role builds is different. Managing a Bozzuto asset sharpens your ability to present financials to sophisticated ownership groups, which is a skill that transfers directly to regional and portfolio-level roles. You'll also develop instincts around client management that most on-site managers never get exposure to until much later in their careers. Strong Property Managers at companies like Bozzuto tend to move into Regional Manager positions with a cleaner track record than peers who came up at operators with less structured reporting expectations.

  • 5+ years in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • Proven P&L and budget management experience
  • Strong client and resident relationship track record
  • Yardi or comparable property management software proficiency
  • Basic knowledge of GAAP
  • Real estate license or willingness to obtain within 90 days
  • Outstanding written and verbal communication

The salary range for this position is $82,000 to $88,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5195743008.title: Property Manager - Wynwood Haus
greenhouse_5195743008.rewritten_description:
Wynwood Haus sits in one of Miami's most design-forward, culturally dense neighborhoods. The residents who live there chose it deliberately. They want a community that matches the energy around it, and they'll notice immediately if it doesn't. That's the context for this Property Manager role at Bozzuto.

This is a full ownership position. You're responsible for the financial performance of the property, the resident experience, the team's development, and the client relationship. Bozzuto works on a third-party management model, which means your reporting to ownership isn't a formality. Accurate, timely financial and operational reports matter here. Clients expect them, and your credibility with ownership depends on delivering them consistently.

Day to day, you're splitting your attention between the numbers and the people. That means reviewing occupancy and traffic trends in the morning, walking units or reviewing a punch list with maintenance, handling a resident escalation before lunch, and then sitting down with your leasing team to talk through conversion rates and concessions strategy in the afternoon. No two days are identical. The role rewards people who can context-switch without losing their footing.

One weekend per month is part of the schedule. That's when prospective residents are actively looking, and Bozzuto expects the manager to be present for it. You'll get two weekdays off in exchange. It's a reasonable trade, but worth knowing upfront.

What separates strong candidates here isn't just P&L experience, though you do need to be comfortable with budget management and a working knowledge of accounting principles. What separates the best candidates is how they lead teams through the unglamorous parts of the job. Rolling up your sleeves, covering gaps, coaching someone through a difficult resident interaction rather than just handling it yourself. That's the culture Bozzuto describes, and it's the culture Wynwood Haus will need.

What Bozzuto is looking for

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least five years of relevant industry experience with a clear leadership track record
  • Demonstrated experience managing revenue, budgets, and P&L
  • Proficiency with Yardi or comparable property management software
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills across residents, vendors, and ownership
  • A Florida real estate license, or the willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire

The salary range is $82,000 to $90,000, with bonus eligibility on top of base. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. Bozzuto has a genuine reputation for investing in career development, and the Property Manager role at a high-profile asset like Wynwood Haus is the kind of position that opens doors to regional or portfolio-level roles over time.

If you're someone who takes real ownership of a community, communicates clearly with clients, and genuinely enjoys developing the people around you, this role is worth a close look.

greenhouse_5145811008.title: Resident Experience Associate
greenhouse_5145811008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto builds and manages properties that people genuinely want to come home to. That standard starts at the front desk, not in a leasing office or a marketing deck. The Resident Experience Associate is the first face prospects see and the consistent presence residents rely on, and that visibility shapes occupancy, retention, and the overall feel of the community in ways that show up in the numbers.

This role runs a non-traditional schedule: Wednesday through Friday from 11am to 7pm, and Saturday and Sunday from 3pm to 11pm. That's not a detail buried in the fine print. It matters. You're covering the hours when residents are actually home, when packages pile up, when someone needs a freight elevator reserved for a move-in, and when a prospect who toured online finally walks through the door. Traffic doesn't stop at 5pm, and neither do you.

Day to day, you're converting inbound leads into scheduled tours, following up with prospects who went quiet, and making sure every interaction feels personal rather than transactional. You'll coordinate with the leasing team to keep tour flow organized, manage front desk logs and package rooms with real precision, and plan resident events that actually get people off their couches. The social media piece matters too: generating authentic content and nudging happy residents toward leaving reviews is a real driver of lease-up momentum and long-term reputation.

What separates a strong candidate here isn't just warmth, it's the combination of genuine people instincts and operational follow-through. Bozzuto's properties run tight. A community that looks impeccable at 11pm on a Saturday didn't get that way by accident. The best people in this role remember names, anticipate needs before they're voiced, and treat a cluttered package room like a problem that reflects on them personally.

What You Bring

  • At least two years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or a similarly customer-facing environment
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and digital tools
  • Willingness to obtain a leasing license within your first 90 days
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds (holiday package volume is real)
  • A detail-oriented mindset and a genuine instinct for anticipating what people need

This role is a natural entry point into property management for someone coming out of service industries. The skills you build here, lead conversion, resident relations, event programming, social engagement, transfer directly into leasing consultant and assistant manager roles over time. Bozzuto operates across multiple markets and property types, so internal mobility is a realistic path for people who perform.

Compensation sits between $43,000 and $46,000 annually, with benefits covering medical, dental, and vision, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5188601008.title: Resident Experience Associate (Thurs-Mon, 3:30pm-12:00am) - $1,500 Sign-On Bonus
greenhouse_5188601008.rewritten_description:
Evening and weekend front desk coverage at a residential community isn't just about answering phones. It's the shift when residents come home tired, packages pile up, maintenance requests get urgent, and the leasing office is closed. Someone has to own that window, and do it well.

Bozzuto is hiring a Resident Experience Associate for their Lynbrook, NY community, working Thursday through Monday, 3:30pm to midnight. The $1,500 sign-on bonus reflects the real value this shift carries. You're not a backup. You're the face of the building during the hours most residents actually live in it.

What This Role Actually Requires

This is a hospitality-forward position, not a pure leasing role, though leasing is part of it. You'll be expected to obtain a leasing license within your first 90 days. Day to day, that means converting inbound leads into scheduled tours, following up with prospects, and contributing to occupancy alongside the core leasing team.

Beyond that, the job is relationship work. Residents expect you to know them. Their names, their preferences, their pets. That kind of familiarity doesn't happen by accident. It requires consistent attention and a genuine interest in people, not just a customer service script.

  • 2 or more years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or travel environments
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and digital tools
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real during the holidays)
  • Availability Thursday through Monday, including weekends and evenings
  • A sharp, professional presentation

You'll also manage front desk logistics: package room organization, club room reservations, freight elevator scheduling, and keeping detailed logs. It sounds administrative, but done poorly it creates friction for every resident. Done well, it's invisible, and that's the point.

What This Role Builds

Strong candidates coming from hotel front desk, concierge, or restaurant management often discover that residential property management rewards the same instincts they already have, just applied over longer resident relationships rather than single transactions. The skills you develop here transfer directly into leasing consultant and assistant manager roles. You'll learn how occupancy goals connect to daily traffic, how resident retention ties to renewal rates, and how consistent community programming supports both.

Bozzuto's platform also means you're not learning in isolation. The company manages a large portfolio of communities, which creates real internal mobility for people who perform. The salary for this role is $45,000, with additional bonus opportunities and a benefits package that includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

If you've built a career around making people feel taken care of, this role gives you a place to apply that craft inside a company that takes its resident experience standards seriously.

greenhouse_5188592008.title: Roving Leasing Consultant
greenhouse_5188592008.rewritten_description:
The Skills This Role Puts to Work Every Day

Leasing is a sales role, and this one is honest about that. As a Roving Leasing Consultant with Bozzuto, you're moving across a portfolio of communities in southern Connecticut (Stamford, Norwalk, Darien) and doing the core work of converting interest into signed leases. That means two distinct skill sets run in parallel here: consultative sales and adaptability. On-call rotation is real here. Evenings and weekends are part of the deal, and you'll need your own reliable transportation to respond to after-hours emergencies. That's a genuine expectation, not fine print.

What You Need to Bring

  • 2 to 5 years of maintenance technician experience, preferably in residential property management
  • HVAC and EPA certification strongly preferred
  • Hands-on expertise in HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair
  • Ability to physically exert 50 to 100 pounds of force occasionally through pushing, pulling, and climbing
  • Solid written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with maintenance software, apps, and digital work order systems
  • Reliable personal transportation for on-call coverage

Where This Role Can Take You

Senior tech roles at companies like Bozzuto are a natural stepping stone toward Maintenance Supervisor or Chief Engineer positions. The skills you build here, specifically systems-level troubleshooting and preventative maintenance program leadership, translate directly to overseeing a full maintenance team at a larger asset or a portfolio of properties. The candidates who move up fastest aren't necessarily the most technically gifted. They're the ones who communicate clearly, document their work, and treat residents the way they'd want to be treated themselves.

The pay range for this position is $28 to $31 per hour, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5197428008.title: Senior Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5197428008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto manages some of the most carefully maintained residential communities in the country, and this Senior Maintenance Technician role at their Redmond, WA property reflects that standard. This isn't an entry-level ticket-clearing position. You're the person the rest of the maintenance team looks to when a control system behaves strangely at 7am or a plumbing issue threatens to escalate into a unit turn nobody planned for.

The day-to-day centers on mechanical, electrical, plumbing, irrigation, and security systems. You're diagnosing problems, adjusting control systems, and staying ahead of deficiencies before residents notice them. Preventative maintenance isn't just a checklist here. It's a program, and you're helping lead it alongside other technicians who need your experience to sharpen their own. When a PM inspection flags something, you're not waiting on it.

Response time matters. Every maintenance request gets addressed within 24 hours, and follow-through with residents, vendors, and the property management team is expected to be professional and consistent. Residents at a Bozzuto community have high expectations, and the maintenance team is a direct part of whether those expectations get met or missed.

The on-call component is real. Rotational after-hours and weekend coverage is part of this role, and you'll need reliable personal transportation to get to the property when something goes sideways overnight. If that kind of unpredictability doesn't fit your life right now, this one probably isn't the right fit.

What the role requires

  • 2 to 5 years of maintenance technician experience, ideally in residential property management
  • Hands-on expertise in HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and/or appliance repair
  • EPA certification strongly preferred; HVAC certification a real plus
  • Ability to exert significant physical force regularly, including pushing, pulling, and climbing
  • Comfort with maintenance software, apps, and digital work order systems

The exposure you get managing preventative maintenance programs and mentoring junior techs builds exactly the operational credibility those next-level positions require. Redmond's competitive rental market means the property stays occupied and active, so you won't be short on experience.

greenhouse_5169130008.title: Senior Maintenance Technician
greenhouse_5169130008.rewritten_description:
What This Role Actually Is

Senior Maintenance Technician at a Bozzuto property in Oakland means you're functioning as the technical backbone of the community. You're not just completing work orders. You're the person other techs come to when the HVAC behaves strangely, when a plumbing issue escalates, or when a control system needs someone who actually understands it. Bozzuto operates across the mid-rise and high-rise tier, so the mechanical systems you'll work on tend to be more complex than a typical garden-style community.

The Day-to-Day

Your scope covers the full range: mechanical, electrical, plumbing, irrigation, and security systems. You'll troubleshoot and adjust control systems, not just swap out parts. Preventative maintenance inspections are part of the rhythm here, and when deficiencies turn up, you're expected to move quickly. Resident service requests get a response within 24 hours. That's a real standard, not a suggestion.

You'll work alongside other maintenance technicians and serve as the senior resource on the team. That means some informal mentoring comes with the territory. On-call rotation is real, including nights and weekends. Oakland properties don't stop needing attention after business hours.

  • 2 to 5 years of residential maintenance experience, ideally in multifamily property management
  • Solid working knowledge of HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair
  • EPA certification strongly preferred; HVAC certification a plus
  • Comfortable with property management software and maintenance apps
  • Reliable personal transportation for on-call response
  • Ability to handle physical demands including lifting and carrying up to 100 pounds occasionally

Building code compliance and fair housing awareness matter here. Bozzuto takes both seriously at the company level, and this role is expected to stay current on local, state, and federal standards.

Where This Role Can Take You

Senior tech is one of the clearest stepping stones in multifamily maintenance. The skills you sharpen here, particularly systems-level troubleshooting and the ability to lead a make-ready process or manage a punch list under pressure, translate directly into a Maintenance Supervisor or Lead Tech role. Bozzuto promotes from within with some consistency, and the company's investment in tuition reimbursement and career development programs is genuine infrastructure for that growth, not just a talking point.

What separates strong candidates from average ones at this level isn't just technical range. It's the combination of technical depth and communication. Residents notice when someone explains what was wrong and what was fixed. Property managers notice when a senior tech closes the loop without being asked. That combination is what gets you considered for the next level.

Compensation for this role runs between $56,000 and $65,000 annually, with bonus eligibility, medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5198602008.title: Senior Property Manager
greenhouse_5198602008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto has built a reputation in multifamily that carries real weight. Their communities tend to skew toward Class A and lease-up situations where resident experience isn't a talking point, it's the actual operating model. This Senior Property Manager role in Miami sits inside that framework, and it's worth understanding what that means before you apply.

The position is a General Manager role in practice. You're responsible for the full picture: occupancy, NOI, team development, client reporting, and the kind of resident satisfaction scores that ownership groups actually read. You'll be coaching leasing and maintenance staff, running performance reviews, and delivering financial and operational reports that clearly explain variances and trends. Bozzuto works with owners and investors, so your communication has to hold up in those conversations, not just internally.

One thing worth naming: weekend availability is part of this role. One weekend per month, with two weekdays off in exchange. That's a real trade-off, and the best candidates for this kind of GM seat have already made peace with the fact that multifamily doesn't pause on Saturdays when traffic picks up.

What separates strong candidates from average ones here isn't budget experience alone. It's the combination of financial fluency and genuine people instinct. Bozzuto's operating model puts a lot of emphasis on how residents feel in the community, which means a GM who can read a T-12 but can't build trust with a team or a difficult resident is going to struggle. The role asks you to coach in the moment, stay hands-on, and treat no task as beneath you. That's a culture signal worth taking seriously.

What You'll Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience, with 5 or more years in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • Demonstrated history of building and retaining high-performing teams
  • Experience managing a budget and P&L, with working knowledge of GAAP
  • Proficiency in Yardi
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills suitable for client and ownership-level audiences
  • Active real estate license, or willingness to obtain one within 90 days of hire

From a career trajectory standpoint, this role is a legitimate stepping stone toward a Regional Manager or Director of Operations seat. GMs who perform well at companies like Bozzuto build a track record with institutional ownership groups, which opens doors that fee management or smaller operators simply can't. The Miami market adds another layer: it's competitive, heavily renter-driven, and gives you exposure to a resident demographic that sharpens your sales and retention instincts fast. The compensation range is $90,000 to $103,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that, plus benefits that include medical, dental, vision, 20 days of PTO, 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5180022008.title: Senior Property Manager
greenhouse_5180022008.rewritten_description:
It's 9 a.m. on a Tuesday and you're already three things at once: reviewing last month's variance report before a client call, coaching a leasing consultant through a tough objection on the floor, and mentally flagging a maintenance trend that's starting to show up in resident reviews. That's the job.

What This Role Actually Covers

Bozzuto is hiring a Senior Property Manager (titled General Manager internally) for a residential community in Washington, DC. This is a full ownership role. You're responsible for the property's financial performance, team development, client relationships, and resident experience.

On the financial side, you'll manage to budget, explain variances clearly in ownership reports, and keep NOI on track. You're expected to know your T-12, understand where the numbers are trending, and communicate that story to clients and investors without hand-holding.

On the people side, you're building and coaching a team. That means real-time feedback, structured performance reviews, and development plans that actually move people forward. Bozzuto expects its GMs to be in the work alongside their teams. No job is beneath you here.

You'll also own the competitive positioning of the community. That includes staying sharp on what comparable properties are doing, working with marketing to drive traffic, and making sure your team can articulate why a prospective resident should choose this community over the one down the street.

One weekend per month is required, with two weekdays off in exchange. Prospective residents shop on weekends, and Bozzuto expects leadership present when it counts.

What You Need to Bring

  • At least 5 years in property management, hospitality, or retail, with demonstrated team leadership experience
  • A four-year degree or equivalent hands-on experience
  • Proven ability to manage a P&L and explain financial performance to ownership groups
  • Proficiency in Yardi and standard property management software
  • Working knowledge of GAAP basics
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills

The salary range for this position is $130,000 to $147,500, plus bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates strong candidates from average ones here isn't just the financial acumen. It's the ability to hold a team accountable while keeping morale intact. DC is a competitive, high-expectation rental market with residents who have options and know it. The GMs who do well at Bozzuto tend to be the ones who treat resident retention as seriously as lease-up, and who build teams that don't fall apart when they're not in the room.

greenhouse_5149840008.title: Senior Property Manager
greenhouse_5149840008.rewritten_description:
It's a Saturday morning. Foot traffic is picking up, your leasing team is running tours, and you're pulling weekend duty, reviewing last night's review responses before jumping in to help close a prospect who's been on the fence for two weeks. That's the rhythm of this role. Not every day looks like that, but many do.

Bozzuto is hiring a Senior Property Manager (General Manager) for a community in Atlanta's 30309 zip code, which puts you squarely in Midtown. That's a competitive submarket with strong Class A demand, high renter expectations, and a steady pipeline of new supply that keeps occupancy and retention conversations real. Residents here have options, and they know it. Your job is to make sure they keep choosing yours.

What This Role Actually Involves

You'll own the full operational picture: financial performance against an established budget, team coaching and development, client reporting, and the day-to-day culture of the property. Bozzuto's model is hospitality-forward, so resident satisfaction scores and community reviews carry real weight alongside your NOI. You'll work directly with ownership groups and investors, delivering accurate financial and operational reports that explain variances clearly, not just present numbers.

Marketing is part of the job too. You'll partner with Bozzuto's consumer marketing team to position the community effectively, and you'll be expected to know the competitive set well enough to coach your team on how to differentiate during every tour and follow-up call.

One weekend shift per month is required, with two weekdays off in exchange. It's worth knowing that upfront.

What You Need to Bring

  • A four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least five years of relevant industry experience, with a real track record of building and retaining strong teams
  • Experience managing a budget or P&L, with solid working knowledge of GAAP basics
  • Proficiency in Yardi and standard industry tools
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, especially when presenting to clients or ownership
  • A Georgia real estate license, or the ability to obtain one within 90 days of hire
  • Experience managing relationships with owners, investors, and vendors

What separates strong candidates here isn't just operational competence. It's the combination of financial fluency and genuine people instinct. Bozzuto's culture puts a premium on how you show up for your team and residents, not just what the numbers say at month-end. If you're the type who sees coaching a leasing consultant through a tough objection as part of your job, not a distraction from it, that mindset fits here.

The salary range for this position is $101,908 to $105,000, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401(k) with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

greenhouse_5182595008.title: Sr. Maintenance Technician - $2,500 Sign-On Bonus
greenhouse_5182595008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto is offering a $2,500 sign-on bonus for a Senior Maintenance Technician at their Harrison, NJ community. If you've put in the hours diagnosing HVAC systems, tracing electrical faults, and closing out work orders before the next shift starts, this role is built around that skill set.

The day-to-day here isn't just reactive work orders. You're taking point on mechanical, electrical, plumbing, irrigation, and security systems, making control adjustments, and catching deficiencies before they become resident complaints or costly repairs. That distinction matters. A strong senior tech thinks in terms of the property's physical condition over time, not just today's punch list. You'll work alongside other technicians to keep the preventative maintenance program on track, which means your organizational habits directly affect how the asset holds up month to month.

Response time is a real KPI in this role. Maintenance requests need a response within 24 hours, and resident follow-up should be professional and timely every time. At a Bozzuto community, the standard of service is high because resident experience ties directly to renewals and occupancy. A tech who communicates clearly with residents and the property management team closes the loop faster and keeps satisfaction scores where they need to be.

One thing worth acknowledging: this role includes on-call rotation for after-hours emergencies, nights, and weekends. That's the reality of residential maintenance at this level. You'll need reliable personal transportation to respond when the call comes in. If that's already part of how you operate, it won't be a surprise.

What Bozzuto is Looking For

  • 1 to 3 years of experience as a maintenance technician, preferably in residential property management
  • HVAC and EPA certification strongly preferred
  • Working knowledge across HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and appliance repair
  • Ability to handle physical demands: occasional exertion up to 100 pounds, frequent lifting and climbing
  • Comfortable with maintenance software and digital work order systems
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Reliable personal transportation for on-call response

The salary range for this position is $55,000 to $57,000, plus eligibility for additional bonus opportunities on top of the sign-on. Bozzuto's benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

For technicians thinking about where this role leads: senior tech experience at a well-run operator like Bozzuto is a direct path toward a Maintenance Supervisor or Service Manager position. You build the diagnostic credibility, the vendor relationships, and the systems knowledge that property managers rely on when they're building a maintenance team from scratch or turning around a struggling asset. That combination of hard skills and operational awareness is what separates technicians who stay senior techs from those who move up.

greenhouse_5190690008.title: Sr. Maintenance Technician - Seaport
greenhouse_5190690008.rewritten_description:
What This Role Actually Involves

Senior Maintenance Technician positions at mid-to-large residential communities aren't for people who want a predictable 9-to-5. This one at Bozzuto's Seaport property in Boston puts you at the front of the technical work: mechanical systems, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, irrigation, and security. You're not just completing tickets. You're the person other technicians look to when a problem gets complicated.

Bozzuto manages and maintains residential communities across multiple markets, and their Seaport property sits in one of Boston's most developed and competitive submarkets. Residents here expect quick, professional service. That expectation lands on you.

The Day-to-Day

A typical week mixes scheduled preventative maintenance work with the unpredictable. You might start Tuesday running through PM inspections on HVAC units, find a control system that needs adjustment, and spend the afternoon troubleshooting before a resident move-in. Thursday could look completely different. That variability is real, and it's worth knowing before you apply.

  • Lead troubleshooting on mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and building control systems
  • Maintain the physical asset with a focus on tasks requiring advanced technical skill
  • Drive the community's Preventative Maintenance Program alongside other technicians
  • Respond to all maintenance requests within 24 hours and follow up with residents and vendors professionally
  • Stay current on applicable building codes, safety standards, and fair housing requirements
  • Participate in on-call rotation, including evenings and weekends for after-hours emergencies

The on-call component is real. Rotational after-hours coverage means some weekends and nights belong to this job. If that's a dealbreaker, it's better to know now.

What Bozzuto Is Looking For

Two to five years of residential maintenance experience is the baseline. HVAC certification (EPA 608) is strongly preferred, and expertise in at least a few of the core trades (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, appliance repair) is expected. You'll need reliable personal transportation for emergency response.

The candidates who do well in senior tech roles tend to combine solid trade skills with genuine communication ability. Residents don't just want a fix. They want to know what happened, what you did, and whether it'll happen again. Written and verbal communication matters here, as does comfort with software and maintenance management apps.

Physical demands are significant. The role requires occasionally moving 50 to 100 pounds, frequently handling 25 to 50 pounds, and constant physical activity through climbing, pushing, and pulling. This is hands-on work throughout the shift.

Compensation and Benefits

The posted salary for this role is $60,000, with eligibility for additional bonus opportunities. Bozzuto's benefits package includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For technicians who want to grow toward a lead or maintenance supervisor role, Bozzuto's size and structure create a real path to get there.

greenhouse_5194139008.title: Weekend Evening Concierge
greenhouse_5194139008.rewritten_description:
It's Saturday at 12:00 PM. You walk in, take over the front desk, and within the first hour you've signed for six packages, helped a prospect find parking, and remembered to ask a longtime resident how her dog's vet visit went. That's the rhythm of this role.

What You'll Be Doing

This is a weekend-only position: Saturday and Sunday, noon to 9 PM. You're the face of the community during those hours. Prospects come in off the street. Residents come home from their day. Everyone gets the same attentive, personalized experience.

The work splits across a few areas. You handle front desk operations: package management, freight elevator scheduling, club room reservations, and keeping logs current and organized. You solve problems as they come up, which means staying calm when something goes sideways and knowing when to escalate. You also plan and execute resident events that actually bring people together, not just put food on a table.

On the social side, you generate content and engagement to keep the community visible online. You also encourage satisfied residents to share their experience through reviews. Both matter for the property's reputation.

What This Role Requires

  • At least 2 years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or a similar customer-facing environment
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and standard computer programs
  • Ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real around the holidays)
  • A professional appearance and genuine attention to detail
  • A customer-first mindset that doesn't require reminding

The pay range is $22 to $25 per hour, with additional bonus opportunities. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

What separates strong candidates here is memory and warmth. Anyone can manage a package log. The people who thrive in concierge roles at luxury residential properties are the ones who remember names, pick up on preferences, and make residents feel genuinely recognized every time they walk through the door. San Francisco residents at this price point have high expectations and plenty of alternatives. Consistent, personalized service is what builds the kind of retention that shows up in renewal numbers.

This position is with Bozzuto, a well-regarded residential operator with properties across major U.S. markets. The role is based in San Francisco at the 94103 zip code.

greenhouse_5186442008.title: Weekend Evening Concierge (4pm - 12am)
greenhouse_5186442008.rewritten_description:
Bozzuto builds and manages residential communities with a reputation for high-touch service, and their concierge roles sit right at the center of that identity. This isn't a gatekeeping position. It's a relationship role, and the person who fills it sets the tone for how residents experience coming home every single day.

What You're Actually Doing on Shift

The shift runs 4pm to midnight on weekends, which means you're covering the hours when residents are most present and most likely to need something. You'll greet people by name, manage package intake and retrieval, handle front desk logistics like club room reservations and freight elevator scheduling, and field whatever comes through the door with composure. Some nights that's a smooth, social few hours. Others involve a frustrated resident, a delayed vendor, and a missing package all at once. You handle it without losing your warmth.

Beyond the desk, you'll help plan and execute resident events that actually bring people together, not just obligatory gatherings. You'll also contribute to the community's social media presence and encourage satisfied residents to leave reviews online. That last piece matters more than it sounds: at a Bozzuto property, online reputation directly reflects the quality of the on-site team.

What You Bring

  • At least two years in hospitality, retail, hotel, or a similar customer-facing environment
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Comfort with software, apps, and property management or front desk platforms
  • A professional appearance and genuine attention to detail
  • Physical ability to lift up to 25 pounds (package volume gets real around the holidays)
  • A customer-first instinct that doesn't require reminding

The hourly range for this role sits between $21.21 and $23.00, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

Who Thrives Here

The candidates who stand out in concierge roles at high-quality multifamily properties are the ones who remember details without being prompted. You know which resident has a dog named Biscuit. You notice when someone looks like they've had a rough day. You don't wait to be asked before solving a problem. That kind of attentiveness is harder to train than most skills on a resume, and it's exactly what a Bozzuto community depends on during evening and weekend hours when the broader management team isn't on site.

This role also builds a practical foundation for growth. Concierge experience at a well-run property gives you direct exposure to resident relations, community programming, and front-of-house operations. Those are transferable skills if you eventually move toward leasing, assistant management, or hospitality management roles. Bozzuto's size and internal development programs make that path realistic, not theoretical.

greenhouse_4203729009.title: Bilingual Resident Services Specialist - Sandy Springs, GA
greenhouse_4203729009.rewritten_description:
Cortland runs one of the larger vertically integrated multifamily platforms in the country, and their centralized contact center model means this role looks a little different from a typical on-site leasing consultant position. You're not walking apartments or collecting keys. You're the voice residents hear when something needs to get resolved, and you're handling that across multiple properties at once.

What This Role Actually Involves

As a Bilingual Resident Services Specialist based in Sandy Springs, you'll work inside Cortland's contact center, fielding inbound calls, emails, and chat messages from residents across the portfolio. The bilingual piece matters here. You'll be communicating in both English and Spanish, which means your ability to de-escalate a tense situation or explain a lease detail clearly doesn't get to stop at one language.

The volume is real. You'll manage multiple conversations simultaneously, toggle between platforms like RealPage, OneSite, and Funnel, and stay organized while priorities shift. Some of what lands in your queue will be simple. Some of it won't. Difficult residents, time-sensitive maintenance concerns, and sensitive account questions are part of the daily mix, not the exception.

  • Handle resident inquiries across phone, email, and chat with accuracy and professionalism
  • De-escalate complaints and document interactions within CRM tools
  • Navigate multiple property management platforms and adapt as systems change
  • Maintain Fair Housing compliance on every interaction, without exception
  • Work collaboratively within the contact center team toward shared service goals
  • Maintain availability for weekend and extended hours as business needs require

What Cortland Is Looking For

Two or more years of customer service experience is required, preferably in a high-volume contact center or multifamily setting. A high school diploma or equivalent is the minimum education requirement. You'll need solid proficiency in Microsoft Office and comfort with property management software platforms. Strong time management isn't a soft skill here. It's a daily operational requirement.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this kind of role is emotional steadiness. It's easy to be pleasant when the call is easy. The candidates who do well here stay calm and solution-oriented when a resident is frustrated, when systems are slow, and when the queue isn't shrinking. Bilingual fluency in Spanish is a core requirement, not a bonus.

The Bigger Picture

Contact center roles at large operators like Cortland tend to build a skill set that translates well across the industry. You'll develop fluency with enterprise-level property management platforms, a strong understanding of resident retention, and experience handling compliance-sensitive conversations at scale. Those skills carry real weight if you later move toward an on-site community manager role or a regional support function. This isn't a dead-end position. It's a clear entry point into a company that promotes from within and has the portfolio size to back that up.

greenhouse_4194354009.title: Bilingual Resident Services Specialist - Sandy Springs, GA
greenhouse_4194354009.rewritten_description:
It's 10:30 on a Tuesday morning. You have a resident on hold who's frustrated about a maintenance delay, two chat windows open with prospective residents asking about availability, and an email queue that grew by six messages while you were handling the last call. That's a normal Tuesday in this role.

What This Job Actually Is

Cortland, one of the largest multifamily owners and operators in the country, runs a centralized contact center model that serves residents across its portfolio from a single hub. This bilingual Resident Services Specialist position, based in Sandy Springs, GA, sits at the center of that model. You're the voice residents hear when something goes wrong, when they have a question about their lease, or when they just need someone to follow through on a promise a previous rep made.

The bilingual piece matters here. You'll serve Spanish-speaking residents who deserve the same quality of communication and resolution that every other resident gets, and your fluency is what makes that possible. Not a translation service. An actual conversation.

Day to day, you're handling inbound calls, emails, and chat inquiries across multiple properties simultaneously. You'll work inside platforms like RealPage, OneSite, and Funnel, so comfort with property management software matters. De-escalation is a real part of this job, not an occasional exception. Some residents are patient. Some aren't. You'll need to hold your professionalism either way, while keeping Fair Housing compliance intact in every interaction.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • Bilingual fluency in English and Spanish, spoken and written
  • At least two years of customer service experience, preferably in a high-volume contact center or multifamily housing setting
  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Demonstrated ability to manage confidential information with discretion
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office and familiarity with CRM and property management platforms
  • Strong organizational skills and the ability to prioritize accurately when everything feels urgent
  • Availability to work weekends and extended hours when the business needs it

What separates the candidates who thrive here from those who burn out quickly is usually one thing: the ability to reset between calls. You'll take a hard conversation, close it, document it accurately, and pick up the next one without carrying the frustration forward. That emotional reset is a skill. If you have it, this role is manageable and genuinely rewarding. If you don't, the volume will wear you down fast.

Cortland's vertically integrated structure means this isn't a third-party management shop passing resident complaints up a chain of vendors. Decisions move faster, and your resolutions tend to carry more weight. That's a meaningful difference for someone who actually wants to close tickets rather than just log them.

greenhouse_4203010009.title: Centralized Painter - Arlington, VA
greenhouse_4203010009.rewritten_description:
Multifamily operators with large portfolios don't leave painting to chance. When you're turning dozens of units a month, inconsistent paint quality slows make-readies, delays move-ins, and chips away at NOI. Cortland, one of the top 10 multifamily owners and operators in the country, runs a centralized paint operation in Arlington, VA to keep that from happening. This role sits inside that system.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Your baseline is one full apartment paint or two touch-up units per day. That's the floor, not the ceiling. You'll handle spray equipment, roller and brush work, and the prep that makes all of it hold up. Small drywall repairs and texture matching are part of the job. You're not waiting for a separate crew to patch before you paint. You handle it.

The paint shop is your responsibility too. That means opening and closing procedures, equipment maintenance, and keeping supplies stocked. Spray equipment that's poorly maintained becomes a scheduling problem fast. You follow the maintenance protocols as written and flag anything that threatens the timeline.

You'll work closely with the Service Manager. When you see a process that could run tighter, you say so. That kind of input is expected here, not just tolerated.

What Cortland Needs From You

  • At least 2 years of interior residential painting experience. Custom home background is a plus.
  • Solid spray equipment skills, including prep and the ability to troubleshoot or repair equipment.
  • Drywall repair capability, including texture matching. Larger project experience is a bonus.
  • Comfort working on ladders and scaffolding.
  • Working knowledge of OSHA and MSDS regulations.
  • Valid driver's license with current insurance.
  • Ability to work independently with minimal oversight.
  • Flexibility for weekend shifts when needed.
  • Maintenance management software experience is helpful. Willingness to learn is required.
  • High school diploma or equivalent.

Strong candidates in this type of role tend to separate themselves through consistency, not speed. Turning a unit fast means nothing if the paint is pulling away from trim or the texture match is off. The painters who stand out here are the ones who bring the same level of attention to unit 14 on a Friday afternoon as they did to unit 1 on Monday morning.

This is a drug-free workplace. Cortland participates in E-Verify.

greenhouse_4192566009.title: Centralized Painter - Boise, Idaho
greenhouse_4192566009.rewritten_description:
Paint is one of the most visible parts of a make-ready, and in a centralized model it's also one of the most measurable. When a company pulls painting out of individual properties and puts it in the hands of a dedicated team, the goal is consistency and throughput. Cortland operates that way in Boise, and they're adding a Centralized Painter to keep units moving.

The production expectation here is concrete: at least one full apartment paint per day, or two touch-up units. That's not unreasonable for someone with solid spray experience, but it does mean you need to be efficient from the moment you open the paint shop. Prep work, equipment maintenance, opening and closing tasks, keeping the shop stocked and clean. These aren't afterthoughts; they're part of the job description because they directly affect your ability to hit daily numbers.

What the Work Actually Looks Like

Most days you're moving between units, running spray equipment, doing roller and brush work where spraying isn't practical, and handling small drywall repairs including texture matching. That last part matters more than people expect. A painter who can't match texture leaves units looking patched, which either delays approval or comes back as a punch list item. The ability to do small drywall work in-house keeps turns moving without waiting on a separate trade.

You'll coordinate closely with the Service Manager on scheduling and flag anything that threatens your timeline. In a centralized setup, one delayed unit can affect the whole day's plan, so communication isn't optional. Working knowledge of maintenance management software is a plus, and if you don't have it, you'll need to pick it up.

What Cortland Is Looking For

  • At least two years of interior residential painting experience; custom home background is a bonus
  • Proficiency with spray equipment, rollers, and brushes, along with proper spray prep
  • Working knowledge of OSHA and MSDS regulations and elevated surface safety
  • Ability to complete small drywall repairs and texture matching
  • Comfort working on ladders or scaffolding
  • Valid driver's license with current insurance
  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Availability including weekends as needed

The candidates who tend to stand out in centralized painting roles aren't just fast. They're consistent. Speed without quality creates rework, and rework destroys the efficiency that the centralized model is built around. If you take pride in a clean cut-in and a properly maintained sprayer, that matters here more than it would in a general maintenance tech role.

Cortland is a top-10 multifamily owner and operator nationally, which means there's infrastructure behind this position. Processes, standards, and resources that smaller operators don't have. For a painter who wants to do the work right without constantly improvising around missing supplies or broken equipment, that's genuinely worth something.
